Convert 6 concepts from card.md to numbered-cards format

Migrate eigendecomposition, svd, symmetric-matrices, backpropagation,
gradient-descent, and convexity to the new cards/NNN.md format:

- Create cards/001.md from each card.md with new metadata header
- Rename 'Core Facts' → 'Facts', 'Key Formulas' → 'Formulas'
- Move Sources into metadata Source field, remove Prerequisites section
- Delete old card.md and additions/ directories
- Update progress.md to per-card schedule format
- Preserve all review history data (eigendecomposition quiz scores)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 6 concept cards + 5 quizzes from learning path

New concept cards:
- linear-algebra: positive-definiteness, orthogonality,
  linear-independence, determinants
- calculus: partial-derivatives, chain-rule

New quizzes (10 questions each, 4 levels):
- SVD, Symmetric Matrices, Backpropagation,
  Gradient Descent, Convexity

Registry updated: 12 concepts across 4 subjects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

6 concepts tracked, 5 unquizzed, all due 2026-04-01.

Priority: re-quiz eigendecomp + first quizzes for all unquizzed by 2026-04-02.

commit.message:
refactor(ez-textfield): #86 make .ez-textfield the sole class for textfield-like inputs

Replace all :where(ez-input, .ez-input, .ez-textfield) selectors with
:where(.ez-textfield) in input.scss, textfield.scss, focus-ring.scss,
and fieldset.scss. Update doc comments and stories to use .ez-textfield
instead of .ez-input/.ez-input.

BREAKING CHANGE: ez-input and .ez-input selectors no longer apply
textfield styles. Use .ez-textfield instead.

commit.message:
refactor(ez-field): #85 use typescale placeholders for input/textfield/field font styles

Replace manual font property declarations with @extend %md-typescale-body-large
(input/textfield text) and @extend %md-typescale-body-small (supporting/help/error
text), aligning with the pattern used by card, badge, list, and dialog components.

Files changed:
- scss/modules/input/textfield.scss
- scss/modules/input/input.scss
- scss/modules/fieldset/field.scss
- ez-field/ez-field.scss

commit.message:
Fix #1929: Install Playwright dependencies in workspace root

Resolved missing @playwright/test module in agent workspaces by:

1. Ran 'npm install --include=dev' in workspace root
2. Installed Playwright browsers with 'npx playwright install chromium'
3. Verified module accessible from all agent workspaces via symlink

Impact:
- QA agents can now run 'npx playwright test' successfully
- Test discovery and configuration works across all 27 test suites
- Unblocks issue #1865 (idle bubbles tests) and future QA work
- Test execution works in CI and local dev environments

See QA_REPORT_1929.md for detailed verification results.

Tracking: #1929

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix XSS vulnerabilities in sub-agent and model name rendering

Escape user-controlled data (agent names, roles, model names) before
inserting into innerHTML to prevent script injection.

- listeners-agents.js: Escape subName and role in addSubAgentCard()
- settings.js: Escape model name in addFallback()

Both files use escapeHtml() from state.js which is loaded first in index.html.

Fixes #1925

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: document scope violation for issue #1922

Grace (designer) was assigned #1922 which requires modifying Go backend
files (internal/server/goal_planner.go), violating her file scope
constraint (electron/renderer/ only).

This is the exact problem #1922 is designed to solve - task assignment
without validating agent file permissions.

Actions taken:
- Documented scope violation on GitHub issue
- Created reassignment issue #1931
- Proposed UI enhancements Grace CAN implement
- Restored unrelated Go changes found in working directory

Recommendation: Reassign to Henry (go-backend) as specified in original issue.

commit.message:
Split office-ipc.js into domain-focused modules (#1913)

Split large office-ipc.js (1848L) into focused modules:
- office-ipc.js (198L) - Main registration & coordination
- office-ipc-agents.js (408L) - Agent operations & management
- office-ipc-interactions.js (591L) - Interaction patterns & conversations
- office-ipc-rooms.js (231L) - Room & space operations
- office-ipc-events.js (532L) - Events, celebrations, activities

Each module handles a specific domain with proper validation.
All IPC handlers preserved, no breaking changes to preload.js API.
Tests pass successfully.

Fixes #1913

commit.message:
feat: add `show-uncovered-lines` option to hide the Uncovered Lines column (#605)

* Initial plan

* feat: add show-uncovered-lines option to hide the Uncovered Lines column

Add a new `show-uncovered-lines` input (default: true) that controls
whether the "Uncovered Lines" column appears in the file coverage table.
When set to false, the column is hidden, reducing table width in PR
comments and step summaries.

* Implement CLI corrections: optional output, TIFF/PNG/DICOM support, PNM piping

1. Optional output file names - derive from input when -o omitted
2. TIFF support (input/output, 8/16/32-bit, LE/BE)
3. PNG support (input/output, 8/16-bit, zlib via CZlib)
4. DICOM support (input only, strip metadata, LE/BE)
5. PNM passthrough piping (stdin/stdout with - convention)
6. Updated help text and usage for all commands

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/Raster-Lab/J2KSwift/sessions/35287ced-c844-4ddc-982c-312dcd7a3a53

- Clean up TIFF BPS placeholder with direct offset computation
- Remove unused DICOM variables (highBit, pixelDataLength)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix MLP underfitting: add gradient clipping and raise lr_init to 1.0 (#42)

* Initial plan

* Fix MLP underfitting: add gradient clipping and increase lr_init to 1.0

Two changes address the flat-line regression on Forrester:

1. Gradient clipping (max_grad_norm=5.0 default): Clips the global
   gradient norm after loss.backward() to prevent the early-training
   oscillation loop where large initial gradients cause oversized
   updates that destabilise the adaptive LR formula. Once the model
   begins fitting, gradient norms settle below the threshold and
   clipping becomes a no-op.

2. lr_init raised from 0.2 to 1.0: With gradient clipping preventing
   oscillation, the full adaptive LR scaling factor can be used,
   giving ~5x faster convergence.

Before: loss ratio 42.3% after 500 epochs (ConstantRF, seed 42)
After:  loss ratio  1.9% after 500 epochs (same config, same seed)

Verified stable across 5 seeds × default architecture.

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/kyleosung/StepByStep-AdaptiveGDForLipschitzNNs_DEV/sessions/73c9c184-8db5-46f8-9b21-3a1c343536c8

Co-authored-by: kyleosung <97492761+kyleosung@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: verify clipping engages, document threshold

feat: fork/join DAG in Graph view with review fixes

Top-level fork/join visualization for parallel subagent branches:
- Turn -> Fork -> Agent Branch[] -> Join DAG structure
- BranchMarkerNode (diamond), AgentBranchNode (dashed rect) renderers
- Time-based edge activation via getEdgeActive + nodeById memo
- Deterministic branch ordering by start time + ELK model order

Review fixes (GPT-5.4 + Claude + manual analysis):
- Fix: mixed turns preserve pre-fork/post-join non-task tools
- Fix: collectDescendants walks full parentToolCallId chain (nested agents)
- Fix: normalizeIdPart appends index suffix to prevent ID collisions
- Fix: getEdgeActive returns false (not true) for unknown nodes
- Fix: fork node endTime spans to joinTime for proper active glow
- Perf: getTurnEvents uses O(1) index map instead of O(n) find scan
- findParallelTaskGroup filters mixed concurrent groups (task + non-task)

Tests: 353 passing (+13 new: fork/join DAG, mixed turns, nested
descendants, 3+ agents, failed branch errors, ID collisions, ordering)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add output standardization to experiment pipeline (#44)

* Initial plan

* Add standardize_targets option to GDTrainer with inverse transform

Adds a `standardize_targets` parameter (default False) to GDTrainer that
standardizes training targets to zero mean and unit variance before
training begins. Stores mean/std for downstream use via the new
`inverse_transform_targets()` method. Handles edge case of constant
targets (std=0) safely. Includes 7 new tests.

* Move standardization from GDTrainer to experiment pipeline utilities

Reverts GDTrainer changes and adds standardize_targets() and
inverse_transform_targets() as standalone utility functions in
src/utils.py. Updates all three experiment pipelines
(stock_experiment, parameter_ablation, training_dynamics_comparison)
to call these utilities before training. Tests updated accordingly.

Use realistic IGC filenames in README and tests

Filenames follow the gliding.net.nz format:
  {last_year_digit}{month_base32}{day_base32}{serial}{flight_base32}.igc

e.g. 641GBE1.igc for 2026-04-01, serial GBE, flight 1.

commit.message:
Set up learning system with spaced repetition and Copilot skills

- Repository structure: concepts/, logs/, learning-path/, analytics/, templates/
- 5 seed concepts: eigendecomposition, SVD, backpropagation, gradient descent, convexity
- Each concept has: card.md (core knowledge), connections.md (knowledge graph), progress.md (SM-2 spaced rep)
- Templates for consistent concept entry creation
- 6 new Copilot skills: concept-card, quiz-master, session-tracker, learning-advisor, analytics-review, concept-reviewer
- 2 existing skills integrated: retrieval-math (live challenges), python-learning-path (implementation projects)
- README with full workflow guide and cognitive science rationale

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Flatten output structure: remove date subfolders

Files are now written directly to {outputDir}/{filename} instead of
{outputDir}/{flight_date}/{filename}.

Stakeholder feedback: all files for an event should be in one folder.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Replace softprops/action-gh-release with gh CLI

softprops/action-gh-release has no Node.js 24 release and is
generating deprecation warnings. Replace with 'gh release upload'
which is pre-installed on all GitHub-hosted runners and has no
third-party action dependency.
